title: GitHub API の 400 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: All major factual claims regarding GitHub API 400 errors, their causes, and example messages are consistent with official GitHub documentation and common API troubleshooting practices.
The advice for resolving issues and seeking further support is appropriate and directs users to official GitHub resources.
The article's claims are up-to-date with current GitHub API practices, as evidenced by the dates of the supporting search results and the article's publication date.

sources.title: GitHub API v3 | GitHub Developer Guide - Libraries for the REST API
sources.claim: Sending invalid JSON will result in a 400 Bad Request response with a message like 'Problems parsing JSON'.

sources.title: GitHub API v3 | GitHub Developer Guide - Libraries for the REST API
sources.claim: The HTTP 400 Bad Request client error response status code indicates that the server would not process the request due to something the server considered to be a client error.

sources.title: GitHub API v3 | GitHub Developer Guide - Libraries for the REST API
sources.claim: For POST, PATCH, PUT, and DELETE requests, parameters not included in the URL should be encoded as JSON with a Content-Type of 'application/json'.

sources.title: REST APIのトラブルシューティング - GitHubドキュメント
sources.claim: If you send invalid JSON in the request body, you may receive a 400 Bad Request response and a 'Problems parsing JSON' error message.

sources.title: REST APIのトラブルシューティング - GitHubドキュメント
sources.claim: If you specify a version that does not exist, you will receive a 400 Bad Request error and a message about the version not being supported.

sources.title: REST APIのトラブルシューティング - GitHubドキュメント
sources.claim: If you exceed your primary rate limit, you will receive a 403 Forbidden or 429 Too Many Requests response.

sources.title: REST APIのトラブルシューティング - GitHubドキュメント
sources.claim: The HTTP `400 Bad Request` client error response status code indicates that the server would not process the request due to something the server considered to be a client error. The reason for a `400` response is typically due to malformed request syntax, invalid request message framing, or deceptive request routing.

sources.title: GitHubの機能や使い方を質問できるコミュニティ「GitHub Community」がオープン - Publickey
sources.claim: GitHub Community (discussions.github.com) is available for users to ask and answer questions and share updates.

sources.title: GitHub Community
sources.claim: GitHub Community (discussions.github.com) is built to support all GitHub users via Discussions.

sources.title: GitHub Support: Sign in for Software Support and Product Help
sources.claim: GitHub Support can be contacted through the GitHub Support portal.

sources.title: 問題用の REST API エンドポイント - GitHubドキュメント
sources.claim: For listing repository issues, the 'state' parameter can be 'open', 'closed', or 'all'.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for issues - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: For listing repository issues, the 'state' parameter can be 'open', 'closed', or 'all'.

sources.title: GitHub API の 429 エラー：原因と解決策 - Zenn
sources.claim: 400 errors are due to problems with the request format itself, so retries are ineffective, unlike 429 errors which are due to rate limiting.

sources.title: API通信で発生する400系・500系エラーの原因と対策 - 霞録
sources.claim: 400 Bad Request means an invalid request (format error or invalid data), with common causes including incorrect JSON format, missing required parameters, invalid data types, encoding errors, empty request body, or API version differences.

sources.title: API通信で発生する400系・500系エラーの原因と対策 - 霞録
sources.claim: When a 400 error occurs, the problem is on the client side, and the way the API is called needs to be reviewed.

sources.title: REST APIのトラブルシューティング - GitLab Docs
sources.claim: A 400 error can occur if required attributes are missing from the API request (e.g., an issue title is not specified) or if attributes do not pass validation (e.g., a user's self-introduction is too long).

sources.title: REST API について - GitHubドキュメント
sources.claim: GitHub API has multiple versions, and endpoint specifications differ by version.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: Consider explicitly mentioning the `X-GitHub-Api-Version` header as the recommended method for specifying API versions in addition to or as a more modern alternative to the `Accept` header, for the most current guidance.
